item.title: Attackers Use AI to Automate EDR Evasion Testing
item.description:
Python scripts were used to test malware against endpoint detection and response agents from Sophos, CrowdStrike, and Windows Defender.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Bugcrowd Launches EU Data Residency Option For Evolving Data Sovereignty Needs
item.description:
Organizations are growing serious about what nation’s rules apply to their data. Experts point to geopolitical tensions as a main contributing factor.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: 4 Critical Threats Where Attackers Have the Advantage
item.description:
Gartner analysts issued a call to action to bolster defenses against several emerging critical threats, such as deepfakes and prompt injections.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: China's TA4922 Expands Cybercrime Attacks Globally
item.description:
One of the world's most diverse, least-focused cybercrime groups is enlarging its footprint beyond East Asia.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Adaptive, Agentic AI Worms Loom as Next Enterprise Threat
item.description:
AI worms, or "viruses with wings and brains," adapt to new environments, seek out vulnerabilities, and will likely strike within a year, researchers say.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Exposed Fuel Tank Gauges Under Attack in the US
item.description:
Threat actors are taking advantage of Internet-exposed tank gauges by breaching gas stations, opening the door to disruption.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Iran Signed a Ceasefire — Its Hackers Didn't
item.description:
An extension of the Geneva Conventions could impose restrictions on cyberwarfare under ceasefire conditions and close a major loophole in international conflict.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Check Point VPN Flaw Exploited Since Early May
item.description:
A newly discovered, critical zero-day vulnerability is under attack; a Qilin ransomware affiliate has been blamed for at least one incident.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Silent Ransom Group Hits US Law Firms in Escalating Extortion Attacks
item.description:
The financially motivated group is combining vishing, IT impersonation, and in-person office intrusions to steal data and extort victims.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: OpenAI Rolling Out ChatGPT Account Security Controls
item.description:
The Active Sessions and Lockdown Mode features are being made more broadly available by the AI giant. The post OpenAI Rolling Out ChatGPT Account Security Controls appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Silent Ransom Group Uses DNS Fast Flux in Attacks
item.description:
Focusing on hacking law firms in the US, the ransomware group relies on fast flux to hide its C&C infrastructure. The post Silent Ransom Group Uses DNS Fast Flux in Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: 174,000 Impacted by Lansing Community College Data Breach
item.description:
Hackers accessed personal information stored on certain Lansing Community College systems in February 2025. The post 174,000 Impacted by Lansing Community College Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Everest Forms Vulnerability Exploited to Hack WordPress Sites
item.description:
The flaw allows attackers to execute arbitrary code remotely and has been exploited in the wild for two months. The post Everest Forms Vulnerability Exploited to Hack WordPress Sites appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Cybersecurity M&A Roundup: 26 Deals Announced in May 2026
item.description:
Significant cybersecurity M&A deals announced by Akamai, Check Point, Cisco, Cyera, Dragos, WatchGuard and Zscaler. The post Cybersecurity M&A Roundup: 26 Deals Announced in May 2026 appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: WhatsApp Catches Spyware Firm NSO Defying No-Hacking Court Order
item.description:
The Meta-owned communications app is filing a federal court contempt order against NSO. The post WhatsApp Catches Spyware Firm NSO Defying No-Hacking Court Order appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Everybody Is Vibe Coding But Nobody Told the Security Team
item.description:
AI-driven development is not something organizations can or should block. But it must be governed. The post Everybody Is Vibe Coding But Nobody Told the Security Team appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: A Security Raises $37 Million for Autonomous Offensive Security Platform
item.description:
The company founded by Yossi Torati, Omer Gull, and Yuval Itzchakov has emerged from stealth mode. The post A Security Raises $37 Million for Autonomous Offensive Security Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Google Patches 5th Chrome Zero-Day Exploited in 2026
item.description:
The vulnerability is tracked as CVE-2026-11645 and it was reported in late April by an anonymous researcher. The post Google Patches 5th Chrome Zero-Day Exploited in 2026 appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: WhatsApp says NSO targeted users with spearfishing attacks in violation of court order
item.description:
WhatsApp said it is filing a federal court contempt order against NSO for violating a permanent injunction that bars it from mounting attacks against its users.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
item.description:
A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
item.description:
An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
item.description:
Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
item.description:
Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
item.description:
Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequ
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
item.description:
The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: $3.3M Settlement Resolves Data Breach Lawsuit Against Mt. Baker Imaging & Northwest Radiologists
item.description:
Mt. Baker Imaging and Northwest Radiologists have agreed to pay $3,300,000 to settle a consolidated class action lawsuit stemming from […] The post $3.3M Settlement Resolves Data Breach Lawsuit Against Mt. Baker Imaging & Northwest Radiologists appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Healthcare Data Breach Statistics – Updated for 2026
item.description:
The HIPAA Journal has compiled healthcare data breach statistics from October 2009, when the Department of Health and Human Services […] The post Healthcare Data Breach Statistics – Updated for 2026 appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Clarinda Regional Health Center Reports Data Breach Affecting 24K Patients
item.description:
Data breaches have been announced by Clarinda Regional Health Center in Iowa, Community Connections in DC, Waveny Lifecare Network in […] The post Clarinda Regional Health Center Reports Data Breach Affecting 24K Patients appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Conduent Business Services Data Breach Affected More Than 62.2 Million Individuals
item.description:
In January 2025, news first surfaced about a massive data breach at Conduent Business Services, a vendor that provides printing, […] The post Conduent Business Services Data Breach Affected More Than 62.2 Million Individuals appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Onsite Women’s Health $2.5M Data Breach Settlement
item.description:
A breach of the email account of an employee of Onsite Women’s Health that exposed the protected health information of […] The post Onsite Women’s Health $2.5M Data Breach Settlement appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Hacking Group Claims Responsibility for Multi-Million-Record DentaQuest Data Breach
item.description:
Wellesley, MA-based DentaQuest, a dental benefits administrator that manages the benefits for 32 million Americans, has announced it is actively […] The post Hacking Group Claims Responsibility for Multi-Million-Record DentaQuest Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Largest Healthcare Data Breaches of 2025
item.description:
2025 was another bad year for healthcare data breaches. As of June, 2026, 2025, 772 healthcare data breaches affecting 500 […] The post Largest Healthcare Data Breaches of 2025 appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Henderson & Walton Women’s Center Settles Class Action Data Breach Lawsuit
item.description:
Henderson & Walton Women’s Center, a Birmingham, AL-based provider of women’s healthcare services, has agreed to settle a class action […] The post Henderson & Walton Women’s Center Settles Class Action Data Breach Lawsuit appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Southern Illinois Ob-Gyn Associates Announces Data Breach Affecting 38,700 Individuals
item.description:
A data breach at Southern Illinois Ob-Gyn Associates has affected 38,700 individuals. Data breaches have also been reported by Wellpoint […] The post Southern Illinois Ob-Gyn Associates Announces Data Breach Affecting 38,700 Individuals appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Over 20,000 Instagram accounts stolen in Meta AI support hack
item.description:
Meta has revealed that 20,225 Instagram users had their accounts hijacked in a recent incident where attackers used Meta's AI-powered support system to reset passwords. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Oxford University discloses data breach after careers platform hack
item.description:
The University of Oxford disclosed a new data breach last week after being informed by its third-party provider, Group GTI, that its CareerConnect career services platform had been compromised. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Check Point links VPN zero-day attacks to Qilin ransomware gang
item.description:
Israeli cybersecurity company Check Point has released security updates to patch a critical flaw affecting Remote Access VPN and Mobile Access deployments, which was exploited in zero-day attacks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Critical UniFi OS bug lets hackers gain root without authentication
item.description:
Attackers can chain three already fixed vulnerabilities in the Ubiquiti UniFi OS server to execute remote code with root privileges and without authentication. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: WhatsApp says it disrupted new NSO spyware phishing attacks
item.description:
WhatsApp has detected and stopped spear-phishing campaigns allegedly conducted by the NSO Group after investigating user reports of social engineering attacks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: New Shai-Hulud attack trojanizes 19 science-focused PyPI packages
item.description:
Hackers compromised 19 packages on the PyPI, collectively downloaded hundreds of thousands of times, in a new Shai-Hulud supply-chain attack that delivered malware designed to steal developer secrets. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: New Apple feature automatically changes your compromised passwords
item.description:
At WWDC 26, Apple announced an Apple Intelligence-powered feature that can automatically fix weak and compromised passwords. This works in Safari, and it's rolling out with iOS 27. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: SoFi confirms third-party data breach at Hong Kong subsidiary
item.description:
SoFi Hong Kong is warning that it suffered a data breach after hackers gained access to a database at a third-party vendor containing customer information. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Most organizations that miss 24-hour patch window report breaches
item.description:
Steve Zurier reports: The Cloud Security Alliance (CSA) found that 80% of organizations that miss the 24-hour patch window report security incidents involving known vulnerabilities. CSA’s study, released June 2, also found that even pre-production controls are not stopping known flaws in the AI age as 82% of organizations lack real-time visibility into AI runtime behavior.... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: KR: Tving CEO Apologizes for Unprecedented Data Leak
item.description:
This is what incident response and accountability should look like in the U.S., too, but almost never does. The Chosun Daily reports: OTT platform Tving, TVING, has faced controversy over leaking members’ personal information, with its representative director personally apologizing. On the afternoon of the 3rd, Tving’s CEO Choi Joo-hee stated, “We sincerely apologize for... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Was “ExPresidents” a real hacker or a fabricated account?
item.description:
DataBreaches recently recommended an article by Alberto Daniel Hill about digital security in Argentina, Uruguay, and Mexico. In describing his article, DataBreaches reported: In one section of his report, Hill calls out a company for allegedly manufacturing cyber threats, which he claims they then use to create public panic through media amplification. With the public... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Ex-Threat Intel Exec Accuses IBM and AT&T of Hiding Hacks
item.description:
Tiffany Wang reports: IBM and AT&T lacked basic security controls and hid nation-state hacking breaches from the government, a former IBM threat intelligence official alleged in a newly unsealed lawsuit. Former IBM Vice President of Threat Intelligence William Barlow claimed the companies did not keep logs for AT&T-managed VPN connections into IBM cloud services and... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Silent Ransom Group (SRG): Uncovering DNS Fast Flux Infrastructure
item.description:
In 2022, DataBreaches wondered whether a group with no name might be the most successful group we had never heard about. Our impression that the group was unique was somewhat confirmed in 2024, when it walked away from a ransom offer of $1.8 million. More recently, the group, now commonly referred to as the “Silent... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Cyberattack closes Evanston Township High School
item.description:
Alice Cooper’s “School’s Out” became the traditional end-of-year song for millions of students since it was first recorded in 1972. But it really is out for summer for Evanston Township High School — at least so far — because of a ransomware attack. ABC News reports that summer school, sports camps, and on-campus activities are... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: FTC Gives Final Approval to Order Against Illuminate Settling Allegations It Failed to Secure Students’ Personal Data
item.description:
From an FTC press release of June 5: Following a public comment period, the Federal Trade Commission finalized a modified order requiring Illuminate Education Inc. to implement a data security program, limit collection and retention of consumer data, and delete unnecessary data to settle charges that the company’s data security failures led to a major... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Instagram Recovery Tool Bug Exposed 20,225 Accounts to Password Reset Abuse
item.description:
Waqas reports: Meta has disclosed a security incident involving an Instagram account recovery tool after attackers used a flaw to send password reset links to email addresses that were not connected to the targeted accounts. According to a data breach notice filed with the Maine Attorney General’s Office, Meta Platforms said the issue affected 20,225 people in... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Essex NHS hospitals records compromised in cyber attack
item.description:
Mason Lewsey reports: Thousands of Essex patient records were compromised in a cyber attack linked to a major NHS data breach, MSE has confirmed. Mid and South Essex NHS Foundation Trust revealed that around 2,380 patient test records were stolen in the attack, which affected data held by third-party provider Synnovis. The trust operates Southend... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: JP: Hokkaido hospitals data leak may hit 510k, HDDs sold online blamed
item.description:
NHK News reports: Japan’s National Hospital Organization says hard drives from two hospitals in Hokkaido were listed on auction sites, resulting in a leak of personal information from at least 180,000 patients and employees. The group warns that the leak could potentially affect up to 510,000 people. Last June, the Hokkaido Medical Center — part... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Unknown
item.description:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Ampex Data Systems Corporation
